title: NBA G-League Call-Up Predictor
description: Engineered an end-to-end G-League call-up prediction system: automated SportsRadar API ETL pipeline aggregating 5 seasons of player stats, contract logs, and roster data; XGBoost and Logistic Regression models achieving 82% recall on a class-imbalanced dataset; and a deployed Streamlit app with interactive call-up simulators and SHAP feature-importance visualizations. Model outputs were independently validated by an NBA scout.

title: Objective Control & Win Probability in League of Legends
description: Analyzed 120,000+ rows of professional esports match data (Oracle's Elixir, 10,053 matches) to determine whether Baron Nashor or Dragon control is the stronger predictor of victory. Applied MNAR missingness analysis, permutation testing (2,000 simulations, p ≈ 0.0), and logistic regression with GridSearchCV — engineering difference features that improved model accuracy from 82.2% (baseline) to 98.9%. Includes fairness testing across game-length subgroups revealing a 26-point accuracy gap on matches under 20 minutes.

title: Global Energy Dashboard
description: Led a 5-person CER UCSD consulting team to answer: does EV adoption measurably reduce oil dependency? Merged IEA oil trade and EV sales data across 50+ countries (1971–2023), applied Log-ARIMA forecasting and logistic S-curve modeling to project adoption through 2035, and quantified ~2.4 million barrels/day in oil displaced by EVs in 2023. Delivered a 6-tab interactive dashboard and 10-year forecast directly to the CER Director for infrastructure planning.

title: NourishOne — Food Pantry Finder
description: Built in 72 hours at a hackathon: a React + Leaflet food access platform aggregating and geocoding 737 verified food pantry locations across DC, Maryland, and Virginia. I led data engineering — cleaning, validating, and structuring the location dataset so the map interface could filter by food type, dietary needs, distance, and language access.

title: Waymo Expansion & SF Transportation Trends
description: Building a 36-month transportation panel (Jan 2023–Dec 2025) merging five public data sources: Waymo CPUC ride records, 4.1M SF taxi trips, Muni monthly boardings, SFO ground trips, and BART exits. Data cleaning and feature engineering complete; EDA and time-series modeling in progress.

title: Boston Bike Map
description: Geospatial visualization of Boston Blue Bikes traffic patterns using D3.js and Mapbox GL JS — showing station-level volume and flow direction across time-of-day filters. Demonstrates layered geospatial rendering and real-time data binding in JavaScript.
